rationale: A visible concentration of armored divisions along the border will deter Tethyan counter-moves and signal resolve to both domestic and international audiences.
Deploying a carrier group to the Eastern Veridian Sea pressures Tethys’s maritime energy exports and secures our southern flank.
Precision strikes on forward logistics hubs could disrupt mobilization timelines without triggering full escalation.
Covert disruption of Tethys’s rail network in border regions will delay troop rotations and expose their logistical fragility.
Targeting energy sector SCADA systems undermines confidence in critical infrastructure and amplifies economic strain.
A staged provocation in a neutral zone could shift diplomatic momentum by framing Novaris as expansionist.
Securing reserves of critical minerals and refined fuels insulates our war economy from Tethyan counter-embargoes.
Quietly shorting Tethys’s currency through third-party actors accelerates capital flight and inflation.
Halting non-essential imports from Tethys erodes their foreign exchange while signaling economic resolve domestically.
Exploit Novaris’ overextended supply lines with decentralized, hit-and-run tactics to erode morale and logistics without direct confrontation.
Secure our border with mobile, irregular units trained for sabotage and ambush, deterring large-scale incursion while conserving resources.
Disrupt Novaris military communications and logistics coordination through targeted cyber operations to delay mobilization.
Cripple Novaris’ revenue stream by covertly targeting key pipeline nodes that transit Tethys-controlled routes, exploiting their dependence on our territory.
Stage plausible incidents that expose Novaris’ aggression to international scrutiny, manipulating global perception without direct attribution.
Deploy deep-cover assets to map command hierarchies and supply vulnerabilities for future precision strikes.
Rally liberal democracies through institutional channels to isolate Novaris diplomatically and legitimize our defensive stance.
Open discreet talks to test de-escalation options while maintaining public resolve, preserving off-ramps if military pressure falters.
Establish protected routes to project moral authority and attract international aid, framing our resilience as a shared democratic cause.
Preemptively secure critical energy and food reserves to insulate the population from embargo shocks and extend economic endurance.
Fast-track alternative export routes and emergency supply deals to reduce dependency on Novaris-linked markets.
Adjust exchange rates to boost export competitiveness and stabilize domestic prices amid growing inflationary pressure.
